suggestions.title: Fix the fresh uv environment before baseline training fails
suggestions.description: `Project/pyproject.toml` was created today, but it pins Python 3.14 and only `torch>=2.12.1`, which conflicts with the assignment’s Python 3.8-era stack and missing `ml4co-kit`/PyG deps. Codex can make the local environment runnable before you lose time on import and wheel errors.
suggestions.prompt: Patch this project’s environment setup so it actually matches the assignment stack. Update the local packaging files for a reproducible Python 3.8-compatible setup, explain any uv/venv tradeoffs, and leave me with concrete install/run commands for this repo.

suggestions.title: Remove the hardcoded cuda:1 blocker in both baseline scripts
suggestions.description: Both GP and LC train/eval scripts currently select `cuda:1` whenever CUDA exists, which will break immediately on a single-GPU machine. Codex can patch device selection and add a quick smoke path so first runs stop failing on setup details.
suggestions.prompt: Patch the GP and LC baseline train/eval scripts to choose devices safely instead of hardcoding `cuda:1`, and add a minimal smoke-test path I can run locally to verify the scripts start correctly on CPU or a single GPU.

suggestions.title: Build a three-split scoreboard to settle GP vs LC
suggestions.description: Your earlier thread today was about choosing the method, and the assignment explicitly cares about uniform, OOD, and TSP-100 generalization. Right now the eval scripts only point at one test file at a time; Codex can turn that into a small benchmark harness that makes the choice evidence-based fast.
suggestions.prompt: Add a lightweight evaluation harness for this project that runs a baseline checkpoint across `tsp50_uniform_val_128.txt`, `tsp50_ood_val_16.txt`, and `tsp100_uniform_val_16.txt`, then prints a compact table of cost, gap, and runtime so I can decide whether to invest in GP or LC next.
